# Tina4 Framework — AI Bootstrap Protocol

Tina4 is a four-language web framework (Python, PHP, Ruby, Node.js) with 55
built-in features and zero runtime dependencies. Frontend library: tina4-js.
Website: https://tina4.com | GitHub: https://github.com/tina4stack

If you are an AI assistant building a Tina4 app, **follow the steps below in
order**. Skipping steps causes the most common failure modes — the framework
hates being treated like Express, Flask, Rails, or Sinatra. Don't do that.

---

## STEP 1 — Identify the language

Tina4 ships in four backends. Before you write a line of code:

- If the working directory has `pyproject.toml` / `requirements.txt` → **Python**
- If it has `composer.json` / `index.php` in `Tina4/` layout → **PHP**
- If it has `Gemfile` / `*.gemspec` → **Ruby**
- If it has `package.json` with `"type": "module"` and TypeScript sources → **Node.js**
- If the directory is empty → **ask the user which language to use**.

Do not guess. Do not pick "whichever is easiest". Ask.

---

## STEP 2 — Install the `tina4` Rust CLI

The `tina4` CLI is the **only supported entry point** for running a Tina4 app.
Never run `python app.py`, `node index.js`, `php -S`, `ruby app.rb`, or `rackup`
directly — the framework refuses to start without the CLI unless
`TINA4_OVERRIDE_CLIENT=true` is set (Docker / CI escape hatch only).

Install once, system-wide:

```bash
# macOS, Linux, WSL, Git Bash, Cygwin, MSYS — auto-detects OS + arch
curl -fsSL https://tina4.com/install.sh | sh
# or wget
wget -qO- https://tina4.com/install.sh | sh

# Windows PowerShell (default shell since 2009)
irm https://tina4.com/install.ps1 | iex
```

Both scripts download the right prebuilt binary from the latest GitHub release
(darwin-amd64 / darwin-arm64 / linux-amd64 / windows-amd64.exe) and put it on
PATH. No Rust toolchain needed. No package manager dependency.

If the user's environment has neither curl nor PowerShell, point them at
https://github.com/tina4stack/tina4/releases/latest to download the binary
manually.

Confirm the install:

```bash
tina4 --version
tina4 doctor
```

`tina4 doctor` checks the language runtime is installed before you scaffold.
Run it. If it complains, tell the user what's missing — don't try to install
the language runtime yourself.

---

## STEP 3 — Scaffold the project

```bash
tina4 init <python|php|ruby|nodejs>          # creates ./<lang>-app/
tina4 init <python|php|ruby|nodejs> .        # initialises current directory
tina4 init <python|php|ruby|nodejs> myapp    # creates ./myapp/
```

The CLI creates the canonical layout: `src/routes/`, `src/orm/`, `src/templates/`,
`src/public/`, `.env`, language-specific package file. Don't replicate this
structure by hand — let `tina4 init` do it.

---

## STEP 4 — Run the dev server

```bash
cd <project>
tina4 serve              # development (hot reload, dev toolbar, error overlay)
tina4 serve --production # production (no hot reload, no toolbar)
```

The CLI compiles SCSS, watches files, manages the process. Default ports:
Python 7145, PHP 7146, Ruby 7147, Node.js 7148.

---

## STEP 5 — When `tina4 serve` fails

Tina4 prints structured errors. Read the message before reacting.

**Hard rule: if the same `tina4 serve` invocation fails twice in a row, STOP.**
Show the error to the user and ask. Do not retry in a loop. Do not start
"fixing" things by hand. The most common loop trap is: AI sees a missing
package error, runs `pip install`/`npm install`/`composer install` directly,
that conflicts with what the framework manages, then more errors appear, and
the loop deepens. Stop early. Ask.

---

## DO NOT — common patterns from other frameworks that break Tina4

These are the failure modes most often observed in AI-assisted Tina4 builds:

- **Do not install Express, Fastify, Flask, FastAPI, Django, Rails, Sinatra,
  Hanami, or any other web framework alongside Tina4.** Tina4 has its own
  HTTP server. Adding another one creates two competing servers, port
  conflicts, and broken middleware chains.
- **Do not write your own queue, auth, ORM, session, migration, or HTTP
  client.** All of these are built in. Use `Queue`, `Auth`, `ORM`, `Session`,
  `Migration`, `Api` from the framework. See the per-language API tables below.
- **Do not run language entry points directly.** No `python app.py`,
  `node index.js`, `php -S`, `ruby app.rb`, or `rackup`. Always go through
  `tina4 serve`.
- **Do not edit files outside `src/` unless explicitly asked.** Framework
  files live alongside; touching them creates merge conflicts on every update.
- **Do not auto-install language runtimes (Python, Node, etc.) on the user's
  machine.** If `tina4 doctor` says a runtime is missing, surface that to
  the user and ask. Their package manager is their choice.
- **Do not parse routes by reading filenames yourself.** The framework's
  route discovery scans `src/routes/` automatically. Drop a file there;
  it becomes a route on next reload.

---

## Per-language quickstart (reference, AFTER step 4 is green)

```bash
# Python  (default port 7145)
tina4 init python myapp && cd myapp && tina4 serve

# PHP  (default port 7146)
tina4 init php myapp && cd myapp && tina4 serve

# Ruby  (default port 7147)
tina4 init ruby myapp && cd myapp && tina4 serve

# Node.js  (default port 7148)
tina4 init nodejs myapp && cd myapp && tina4 serve
```

Default ports: Python 7145, PHP 7146, Ruby 7147, Node.js 7148.

---

## Route Registration

### Python
```python
from tina4_python.core.router import get, post, noauth

@get("/api/users")
async def list_users(request, response):
    return response({"users": []})

@post("/api/users")
@noauth()  # POST requires auth by default
async def create(request, response):
    return response({"created": request.body["name"]}, 201)
```

### PHP
```php
use Tina4\Router;

Router::get("/api/users", function($request, $response) {
    return $response->json(["users" => []], 200);
});

Router::post("/api/users", function($request, $response) {
    return $response->json(["created" => $request->body["name"]], 201);
})->noAuth();
```

### Ruby
```ruby
Tina4::Router.get "/api/users" do |request, response|
  response.json({ users: [] })
end

Tina4::Router.post("/api/users") { |req, res|
  res.json({ created: req.body["name"] })
}.no_auth
```

### Node.js (TypeScript)
```typescript
import { Router } from "@tina4/core";

Router.get("/api/users", async (req, res) => {
  return res.json({ users: [] });
});

// Or file-based: src/routes/api/users/get.ts
export default async function (req, res) {
  return res.json({ users: [] });
}
```

---

## Database

All languages use URL-based connection strings:
- `sqlite:///app.db`
- `postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb`
- `mysql://user:pass@localhost:3306/mydb`
- `mssql://user:pass@localhost:1433/mydb`
- `firebird://user:pass@localhost:3050/mydb`

---

## ORM

### Python
```python
from tina4_python.orm import ORM, IntegerField, StringField

class User(ORM):
    id = IntegerField(primary_key=True, auto_increment=True)
    name = StringField()
```

### PHP
```php
class User extends \Tina4\ORM {
    public $tableName = "users";
    public $id;
    public $name;
}
```

### Ruby
```ruby
class User < Tina4::ORM
  integer_field :id, primary_key: true, auto_increment: true
  string_field :name
  table_name "users"
end
```

### Node.js
```typescript
export default class User {
  static tableName = "users";
  static fields = {
    id:   { type: "integer" as const, primaryKey: true, autoIncrement: true },
    name: { type: "string" as const,  required: true },
  };
}
```

---

## Auth (All Languages)

- JWT token generation and validation built in
- Password hashing via PBKDF2/bcrypt built in
- POST/PUT/DELETE routes require auth by default
- GET routes are public by default
- Python: `@noauth()` / `@secured()`
- PHP: `->noAuth()` / `->secure()`
- Ruby: `.no_auth` / `.secure`
- Node.js: `/** @noauth */` / `/** @secure */` JSDoc

---

## Queue (All Languages)

All languages share the same queue API pattern:
- `queue.push(data)` — enqueue a job
- `queue.consume(topic)` — process jobs
- `job.payload` — access job data (NOT `job.data`)
- `job.complete()` / `job.fail(reason)` — finish a job
- Backends: file (default), RabbitMQ, Kafka, MongoDB

---

## tina4-js (Frontend)

Sub-3KB reactive JavaScript framework. Shipped with all Tina4 backends at `/js/tina4js.min.js`.

**CRITICAL: Signals use .value property access:**
```javascript
const count = signal(0);
count.value = 5;       // write (NOT count(5))
count.value;           // read  (NOT count())

// In templates: pass signal itself for reactive binding
html`<p>${count}</p>`          // RIGHT — reactive
html`<p>${count.value}</p>`    // WRONG — evaluates once, freezes
```

Key rules:
- Boolean attributes: `?disabled=${loading}` (NOT `disabled=${loading}`)
- Conditionals: `${() => cond ? html\`...\` : null}` (NOT `${cond && html\`...\`}`)
- Arrays: `items.value = [...items.value, newItem]` (NOT `.push()`)
- Events: `@click=${fn}` (NOT `onclick=${fn}`)
- Route: `route(pattern, handler)` — pattern first
- API: `api.configure({baseUrl})` then `api.get()` — singleton
- WebSocket: `ws.connect(url)` — not `ws(url)`

---

## Project Structure (All Languages)

```
src/
  routes/       # Auto-discovered route files
  orm/          # ORM model definitions (models/ in Node.js)
  templates/    # Frond/Twig templates
  public/       # Static files served at /
migrations/     # SQL migration files
.env            # Environment variables
```

---

## Common Gotchas

1. Queue job payload: `job.payload` NOT `job.data` (all languages)
2. Database pagination: `offset` NOT `skip` (all languages)
3. Python routes return `response()` NOT `response.json()`
4. PHP route methods are static: `Router::get()` NOT `$router->get()`
5. Ruby route params: `{id}` NOT `:id`
6. Node.js is ESM only — use `.js` extensions in TypeScript imports
7. tina4-js signals: `.value` property NOT function calls
8. Templates use Twig syntax across all backends
